data.title: Morgan Advanced Materials Grants Performance-Linked LTIP Awards to Senior Executives
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Morgan Advanced Materials ( (GB:MGAM) ) just unveiled an update.

Morgan Advanced Materials has granted a series of conditional long-term incentive plan (LTIP) awards in the form of ordinary shares to several senior executives, including the CEO, CFO and other key PDMRs, under its 2022 share plan. The awards, made off-market on 26 March 2026 at a reference price of £1.929 per share, will vest over three years subject to performance conditions, further aligning leadership remuneration with shareholder value and long-term strategic targets.

The largest allocation went to chief executive Damien Caby, alongside substantial grants to CFO Richard Armitage and divisional and functional heads across HR, legal, IT and core product lines, signalling a broad-based incentive structure at the top of the organisation. The move underscores the company’s continued reliance on equity-linked pay to retain and motivate senior management amid competitive industrial markets and to reinforce accountability for delivering its multi-year performance objectives.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:MGAM) stock is a Buy with a £207.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Morgan Advanced Materials stock, see the GB:MGAM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MGAM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MGAM is a Neutral.

The score is led by mixed fundamentals: strong and improving cash flow is a clear positive, but recent revenue/profit deterioration and meaningful leverage constrain the financial profile. Technicals are notably weak with the stock in a downtrend, partially offset by supportive valuation (low P/E and high dividend). Earnings call guidance suggests modest improvement, but ongoing semiconductor/segment weakness and higher financing costs keep risk elevated.

To see Spark’s full report on MGAM stock, click here.

More about Morgan Advanced Materials

Morgan Advanced Materials is a UK-listed engineering group specialising in advanced materials and components, including technical ceramics and thermal products, supplied into industrial, energy, transport and other performance-critical markets. The company focuses on high-specification solutions that support customers in demanding operating environments and applications worldwide.

data.title: Unilever Spins Off Foods in $44.8bn McCormick Tie-Up to Sharpen HPC Focus
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Unilever ( (GB:ULVR) ) has issued an announcement.

Unilever has agreed to combine its Foods business with McCormick & Company, creating a $20 billion-revenue global flavour group spanning herbs, spices, seasonings, cooking aids, sauces and condiments. The combined business, which will retain the McCormick name and leadership while adding senior Unilever Foods executives, will own iconic brands such as McCormick, Knorr and Hellmann’s and is targeting about $600 million in annual cost synergies within three years.

The deal values Unilever Foods at $44.8 billion and will see Unilever and its shareholders receive 65% of the combined company’s equity plus $15.7 billion in cash, while McCormick shareholders will hold 35%. The transaction, expected to close by mid-2027 subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals, will refocus Unilever as a pureplay home and personal care group with a higher structural growth profile, stronger premium and emerging-market exposure, and capital returned to investors via debt reduction and a planned €6 billion share buyback.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:ULVR) stock is a Buy with a £5062.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Unilever stock, see the GB:ULVR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ULVR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ULVR is a Outperform.

The score is driven mainly by steady profitability and reliable free cash flow, tempered by higher leverage. Technicals are supportive (price above key averages, positive MACD) but appear overextended on RSI/Stoch. Valuation is somewhat demanding at ~22.7x earnings, while the ~3.44% dividend yield and earnings-call outlook (cautious growth guidance plus buyback and cash generation) provide support.

To see Spark’s full report on ULVR stock, click here.

More about Unilever

Unilever is a global consumer goods group with a major presence in beauty, wellbeing, personal care and home care. The company focuses on high-performing, innovative brands across fast-growing categories and geographies, with significant exposure to emerging markets and digital commerce channels.

Average Trading Volume: 4,289,707

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £98.96B

data.title: Central Asia Metals Updates Market on Total Voting Rights
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Central Asia Metals ( (GB:CAML) ) has issued an update.

Central Asia Metals has confirmed that as of 31 March 2026 its issued share capital stands at 177,904,281 ordinary shares of US$0.01 each, of which 193,325 shares are held in treasury. This leaves a total of 177,710,956 voting rights, a key reference figure for investors monitoring their disclosure thresholds under the UK Financial Conduct Authority’s transparency rules.

The announcement provides clarity for shareholders and market participants on the company’s current capital and voting structure, which is important for regulatory reporting and for assessing ownership changes in the AIM-quoted miner. By updating the market on its voting rights, Central Asia Metals supports compliance with disclosure requirements and maintains transparency around its shareholder base and corporate governance framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:CAML) stock is a Hold with a £173.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Central Asia Metals stock, see the GB:CAML Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CAML Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CAML is a Neutral.

The score is supported by a conservatively financed balance sheet and resilient cash generation, reinforced by management commentary on strong EBITDA/FCF and continued dividends. Offsetting this are elevated earnings volatility (including the impairment-driven loss) and clearly bearish technical signals with the stock trading below key moving averages.

To see Spark’s full report on CAML stock, click here.

More about Central Asia Metals

Central Asia Metals is a London-based, AIM-quoted mining company focused on base metals, owning 100% of the Kounrad SX-EW copper operation in central Kazakhstan and the Sasa zinc-lead mine in North Macedonia. The group also holds an 80% stake in CAML Exploration for early-stage projects in Kazakhstan and a 32.6% interest in Aberdeen Minerals, which is developing base metals opportunities in northeast Scotland.

data.title: TransThera Sciences Widens Annual Loss Amid Continued R&D Spend
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The latest announcement is out from TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc. ( (HK:2617) ).

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc., a Hong Kong-listed biopharmaceutical developer, reported no revenue for 2025 as it continued to focus on its research pipeline. The company’s cost base is dominated by research and development and administrative expenses, underscoring its status as an R&D-driven business without marketed products.

For the year ended 31 December 2025, the group posted a net loss of RMB295.96 million, widening from RMB274.61 million a year earlier, driven mainly by sustained high R&D spending and higher administrative costs. Other income and gains declined year-on-year, while basic and diluted loss per share increased to RMB0.76 from RMB0.72, highlighting ongoing cash burn and underlining execution and funding risks typical for pre-commercial biotech issuers.

More about TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc.

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc. is a Hong Kong-listed biopharmaceutical company focused on drug discovery and development. The group operates as a pre-revenue enterprise, with its activities centered on research and development rather than commercial sales, reflecting its positioning in the early- to mid-stage innovative therapeutics segment.

data.title: China Merchants Bank to Fully Redeem RMB27.5 Billion Domestic Preference Shares in 2026
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from China Merchants Bank Co ( (HK:3968) ).

China Merchants Bank has announced that it will fully redeem its 275 million domestic preference shares, issued in December 2017 for total proceeds of RMB27.5 billion, on 15 April 2026. The bank will repay the nominal value of RMB100 per share plus accrued dividends calculated from 18 December 2025 to 14 April 2026 according to the preset dividend rate and formula.

The redemption has been approved under existing shareholder authorizations, with delegated directors Wang Liang and Zhu Eric Liwei jointly signing the decision, and the National Financial Regulatory Administration issuing a no‑objection reply. This move will retire a substantial tier of hybrid capital, potentially simplifying the bank’s capital structure and may affect investors holding these preference shares, who will receive cash redemption and dividends instead of ongoing coupon income.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3968) stock is a Buy with a HK$53.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Merchants Bank Co stock, see the HK:3968 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Merchants Bank Co

China Merchants Bank Co., Ltd. is a major Chinese commercial bank, listed in Hong Kong with H shares, providing a broad range of banking and financial services. The bank raises capital through instruments such as domestic preference shares and focuses on maintaining regulatory compliance and optimizing its capital structure in mainland China and Hong Kong.

Average Trading Volume: 16,267,785

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$1146.9B

data.title: Golden Solar New Energy Posts Wider 2025 Loss on Slumping Revenue and Higher Costs
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Golden Solar New Energy Technology Holdings Limited ( (HK:1121) ) has issued an update.

Golden Solar New Energy Technology Holdings Limited reported a steep decline in revenue to RMB155.1 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, down from RMB253.5 million in 2024, as rising costs pushed the Group into a gross loss of RMB2.4 million compared with a gross profit a year earlier. The company booked higher general and administrative expenses, continued research and development spending, and additional impairment losses on inventories and property, plant and equipment, resulting in an expanded net loss attributable to shareholders of RMB307.9 million and a wider basic loss per share of 16.763 RMB cents, underscoring ongoing operational and profitability challenges for stakeholders.

Finance costs increased year-on-year and the Group also recorded a small share of loss from an associate and a fair value loss on investment properties, while tax expenses remained minimal. The deeper full-year loss signals persistent pressure on the company’s margins and balance sheet, highlighting the need for cost discipline and improved revenue quality as it seeks to strengthen its position in the competitive solar and new energy market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1121) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Golden Solar New Energy Technology Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1121 Stock Forecast page.

More about Golden Solar New Energy Technology Holdings Limited

Golden Solar New Energy Technology Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the new energy and solar technology sector through its subsidiaries. The Group focuses on manufacturing and selling solar-related products and technologies, positioning itself within the broader renewables and clean energy equipment market.

AIA Group Limited has announced the current composition of its board of directors, led by independent non-executive chairman Sir Mark Edward Tucker and executive director Lee Yuan Siong, the group chief executive and president. data.title: Soma Gold’s Colombian Unit Renews Carbon Neutrality Certification for Second Year
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Soma Gold ( (TSE:SOMA) ) has shared an announcement.

Soma Gold’s Colombian subsidiary, Operadora Minera SAS, has renewed its carbon neutrality certification for the 2024 reporting period, marking a second consecutive year of achieving this status. The certification, verified by Bureau Veritas under the PAS 2060:2014 standard, confirms carbon neutrality for direct Scope 1 emissions and Scope 4 emissions associated with products used by the organization.

For 2024, Operadora recorded 1,367.40 tonnes of CO2e emissions, all of which were fully offset through verified carbon credits sourced from the Galilea Amé forest conservation project in Colombia. The company remains the first and only mining operator in the country to hold this certification, underscoring its environmental leadership in a sector under growing pressure to decarbonize.

Soma’s sustainability strategy is built on the use of 100% renewable hydroelectric power, operational efficiency measures, and carbon offsets via REDD+ conservation projects in Colombia. The renewed certification reinforces Soma’s positioning as a responsible miner, which could enhance its appeal to environmentally focused investors, local communities, and regulators concerned with climate impact.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:SOMA) stock is a Hold with a C$2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Soma Gold stock, see the TSE:SOMA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SOMA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SOMA is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mixed financial performance: strong operating margins and solid cash conversion are offset by declining revenue, falling free cash flow, and high leverage. Technicals are supportive due to a clear uptrend, but overbought momentum indicators raise near-term pullback risk. Valuation is only fair given the P/E and lack of dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on SOMA stock, click here.

More about Soma Gold

Soma Gold Corp. is a profitable gold mining and exploration company focused on assets in Antioquia, Colombia, where it controls over 430 square kilometers of mineral concessions along the prolific OTU fault. It operates two fully permitted mills, El Bagre and El Limon, with a combined capacity of 675 tonnes per day and funds a regional exploration program from internal cash flow. The company also holds an exploration property near Tucuma in Brazil under option to Ero Copper Corp. Soma emphasizes sustainability and community engagement as core elements of its operational strategy.

Average Trading Volume: 251,614

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$200.2M

See more insights into SOMA stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
